title: Eval Loop Strategy Briefing
summary: Notes comparing weekly human review, automated scoring, holdout cases, and release guardrails. Briefing focus: Recommend whether to expand automated evals, balancing coverage, citation support, trace utility, and cost.

claims.text: Quality review memo: Teams need smaller eval batches with visible failure clusters before expanding model coverage.
Release guardrail notes: Coverage can improve while grounding regresses, so citation support needs an explicit shipping gate.
Cost review: Long traces help debug failures, but unchecked retries created a 1.34x cost increase in the last dry run.

openQuestions: Confirm whether holdout protocol has newer follow-up data.
Confirm whether reviewer retro notes has newer follow-up data.
recommendation: Use this as a fast first pass, then inspect source tension and citation support in the lab before treating the recommendation as shippable.
